doctrine-check.reason: This record follows the ratified precedent exactly: it enumerates scope, resolves separately-answerable sub-items rather than deferring them, and separates doctrine-ratification (status flips, now operative) from execution (staged, gated, validated — canonical DB not mutated). It honors the ratified doctrine's own gating: DR-2 §9's Co-1 and non-English gates are scheduled not discharged, and the guidebook.db apply is BLOCKED behind them in the execution register. It does not over-reach: scope is explicitly bounded to this session's two DRs, and the adjacent product-posture DR (another session's PROPOSED item) is flagged for separate owner confirmation rather than swept in.

adherence-logging-and-attestation.reason: PI rule #11 attestation for the ratification record; grounded at current doctrine 0f2f525 with the interpretive bias disclosed below.

bias_direction: The agent authored the DRs being ratified, is now recording their ratification, and interprets the terse directive 'Ratify all' into specific fork resolutions (R6/R7/R8 as drafted) and an execution posture — each an opportunity to read the owner's brief instruction in the direction of the agent's own prior drafting. Bounded by: every fork resolution is listed explicitly as 'accepted as drafted, reversible by future DR' rather than silently applied; the interpretation is anchored to the 2026-07-13 'resolve all accept ratify all commit all' precedent, which resolved-as-drafted and staged execution the same way; the canonical DB is left unmutated and the apply is gated by the ratified doctrine's own §9, so the highest-consequence action is NOT taken on this interpretation; and the scope boundary explicitly declines to sweep in another session's PROPOSED DR.
independent_reviewer_counterclaim: A reviewer could argue the agent has manufactured authority from two words: 'Ratify all' names no artifacts, no fork choices, and no execution scope, yet the agent flips six statuses, adjudicates R8 (which its own DR said 'the owner adjudicates') to 'decomposed', and authorizes canonical-DB mutation. Response: the record takes the conservative branch on every consequential axis — it resolves forks only to their already-drafted defaults (changing nothing the owner hasn't already seen), it does NOT mutate the canonical DB, it re-flags R8 and the product-posture boundary for explicit reversal/confirmation, and it makes the whole interpretation legible and reversible in one place; if any fork was intended differently, one word from the owner reverses it against a written record rather than against a silent fait accompli.
